metadata.author: Evan (econometrics) + Dada (implementation)
metadata.description: Mapping rules for Environment Interaction Radar scores (HY-IG spread vs SPY). All scores are on a 0–5 scale.
notes: All thresholds and formulas are confirmed by Evan (Econometrics Agent) on 2026-03-10.
Caps and normalization constants (e.g. 0.10 for beta_garch, 0.03 for transfer entropy) are calibrated from observed magnitudes in econometrics_summary_20260228.md.
If future data materially changes these magnitudes, update the caps here and record the rationale in this file.

correlation_score.description: Absolute Pearson correlation between HY–IG credit spread signal and SPY forward returns, aggregated at the multi-week to multi-month horizon. The raw correlation_value in environment_interaction_scores_hy_ig_spy.json is the primary input.
correlation_score.transform: Take absolute value of the correlation estimate: abs_r = abs(correlation_value). If correlation_value is missing, treat abs_r = 0.

thresholds.interpretation: Very weak correlation; effect size too small for practical use.
Moderate correlation; relationship is noticeable at longer horizons.
Strong correlation; credit spread and equity returns co-move meaningfully.
Very strong correlation; unusually tight linear co‑movement.

lead_lag_score.description: Lead/lag timing advantage based on cross‑correlation (CCF) between HY–IG spread changes and SPY returns. The lead_days input is the effective number of calendar days HY–IG tends to move before SPY when the cross‑correlation is strongest.
lead_lag_score.transform: Compute the CCF across integer lags (in trading days). Identify the lag with the maximum absolute CCF where significant_95 is True. If this lag <= 0, set lead_days = 0 (contemporaneous or equity‑led). If this lag > 0, set lead_days = lag. If no significant lags exist or data are missing, set lead_days = 0.

thresholds.interpretation: No reliable lead advantage; credit and equity move together or equity slightly leads.
Very short lead (1–3 days); may help for tactical timing but sensitive to noise.
Short‑term lead (4–10 days); potentially meaningful tactical edge.
Medium‑term lead (11–30 days); strong evidence of predictive timing.
Very long lead (>30 days); unusually strong timing advantage.

stress_sensitivity_score.description: Composite, regime‑aware measure of how strongly HY–IG spread changes are linked to equity stress, volatility spikes, and strategy performance in stress regimes.

inputs: beta_garch: economic magnitude of the hy_ig_spread_chg coefficient in the GJR‑GARCH mean or variance equation (daily SPY return response per 1 bps of spread).
GARCH_effect.interpretation: How much SPY returns react to unit changes in HY–IG spread; capped to avoid extreme leverage by a few outlier estimates.

inputs: maxDD_stress: maximum drawdown of SPY in stress regime (Q4_stress).
maxDD_calm: maximum drawdown of SPY in calm regime (Q1_calm).
Regime_Asymmetry.interpretation: How much worse drawdowns are in stress vs calm when HY–IG is elevated; capped at 60 percentage points of drawdown difference.

inputs: stress_sharpe_diff = mean over stress windows of (HMM_long_sharpe - BH_sharpe).
Stress_Strategy_Benefit.interpretation: How much a HY–IG overlay improves Sharpe during stress episodes relative to buy‑and‑hold; negative differences are floored at 0.

stress_sensitivity_score.composite_normalization: Each component is first normalized to [0, 1]. The weighted average CompositeScore is by construction in [0, 1]. If any component is missing or NaN, it is treated as 0 in the composite.

score_bands.interpretation: Essentially no link between HY–IG and equity stress.
Weak stress linkage; effect visible but not dominant.
Moderate stress linkage; credit spreads matter but are not the only driver.
High stress linkage; HY–IG is a strong stress indicator.
Very high stress linkage; equity stress is closely tied to HY–IG behavior.
Extreme stress linkage; HY–IG behaves almost like a direct stress state flag.

causality_score.description: Regime‑aware composite measure of directional causality from HY–IG spreads to SPY, combining Toda‑Yamamoto tests, transfer entropy, and state‑dependent local projections. Emphasis is on stress‑regime causality rather than full‑sample Granger causality.

Toda_Yamamoto_score.formula: Toda_Yamamoto_score = 1.0 if p_credit_to_equity_stress < 0.05; 0.5 if p_credit_to_equity_stress < 0.10; 0.0 otherwise.
inputs: p_credit_to_equity_stress: p‑value for Credit→Equity null in the stress subsample.
Toda_Yamamoto_score.interpretation: Binary/ordinal measure of whether HY–IG has statistically significant predictive power for SPY in stress regimes.

inputs: TE_credit_to_equity: estimated transfer entropy from HY–IG to SPY at the chosen lag (typically lag=1).
TransferEntropy_norm.interpretation: Information‑theoretic strength of directional dependence; cap reflects the typical observed scale (≈0.02).

inputs: t_stat_LP: t‑statistic for the state‑dependent credit‑in‑stress term (e.g. spread_chg_x_stress) in local projections.
LocalProjections_component.interpretation: How strong the estimated forward effect is once controls and regimes are accounted for; values beyond |t|=3 are treated as fully strong (component=1).

Regime_activation_flag.formula: Regime_activation_flag = 1.0 if causality appears in stress but not in calm (p_stress < 0.05 and p_calm >= 0.05); otherwise 0.0.
Regime_activation_flag.interpretation: Flags whether the causal channel is specifically a stress‑regime phenomenon, rather than full‑sample.

causality_score.composite_normalization: Each component is in [0, 1], so CompositeCausality is in [0, 1]. Missing or NaN components are treated as 0.

score_bands.interpretation: No meaningful evidence of directional causality.
Weak or unstable causal evidence; may not be reliable out of sample.
Moderate causal evidence; effect exists but is not dominant.
Strong causal evidence in stress regimes, with reasonable robustness.
Very strong causal channel; HY–IG reliably leads SPY in stress periods.
Extreme, highly confident causal link; rare in macro‑finance data.

causality_score.stress_significance_rule: To assign a causality_score >= 3, require stress‑subsample significance: p_credit_to_equity_stress < 0.05. If this condition is not met, clamp the score to at most 2 regardless of CompositeCausality.

hy_ig_spread_to_SPY.description: Illustrative application of the mapping rules to HY–IG spread vs SPY using 2026‑02‑28 evidence files. Values are approximate and for audit intuition; the reproduce_score.py script should be treated as the canonical computation.
